q.en: In what century was the steam engine invented?
q.zh: 蒸汽机是在哪个世纪发明的？

explain.en: The book says: 'In the 1700s, the invention of the steam engine changed everything.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'在18世纪，蒸汽机的发明改变了一切。'

q.en: Who built the first steam engine that worked really well, in 1712?
q.zh: 谁在1712年制造了首台真正运转良好的蒸汽机？

explain.en: The book says: 'Almost 300 years ago, in 1712, Thomas Newcomen built a steam engine. Others had built steam engines, but Newcomen's was the first to work really well.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'将近300年前的1712年，托马斯·纽科门制造了一台蒸汽机。其他人也造过，但纽科门的机器是首台真正运转良好的。'

q.en: What did Newcomen use his steam engine to do?
q.zh: 纽科门用他的蒸汽机做什么？

options.en: Pump water out of coal mines

explain.en: The book says: 'He used his steam engine to run a water pump, which pumped water out of coal mines.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'他用蒸汽机驱动水泵，把水从煤矿中抽出来。'

q.en: What was the main problem with Newcomen's steam engine?
q.zh: 纽科门的蒸汽机主要问题是什么？

options.en: It needed a large amount of coal to do a small amount of work
options.zh: 它需要用大量的煤来完成少量的工作

options.en: It was too small to be useful

explain.en: The book says: 'The engine had one problem. It needed a large amount of coal to do a small amount of work.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'这台机器有一个问题：它需要用大量的煤来完成少量的工作。'

q.en: How did James Watt improve the steam engine in the 1760s?
q.zh: 18世纪60年代，詹姆斯·瓦特如何改进了蒸汽机？

options.en: He made it work with less coal
options.zh: 他让机器用更少的煤工作

options.en: He used wood instead of coal

explain.en: The book says: 'In the 1760s, Scotsman James Watt improved Newcomen's engine. He made the machine work with less coal.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'18世纪60年代，苏格兰人詹姆斯·瓦特改进了纽科门的机器。他让机器用更少的煤工作。'

q.en: What larger historical event did Watt's rotary steam engine help lead to?
q.zh: 瓦特的旋转式蒸汽机促成了哪场更大的历史事件？

options.en: The Industrial Revolution, also called the Age of Steam
options.zh: 工业革命，又称蒸汽时代

explain.en: The book says Watt's rotary engine 'was one event that led to the Industrial Revolution, also known as the Age of Steam.'
explain.zh: 书中说瓦特的旋转式蒸汽机'是导致工业革命（又称蒸汽时代）的事件之一'。

q.en: Who built one of the first steam locomotives, or trains, in 1804?
q.zh: 谁在1804年制造了最早的蒸汽机车（火车）之一？

explain.en: The book says: 'Trevithick built one of the first steam locomotives, or trains, in 1804. It hauled coal at a factory.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'特雷维西克于1804年制造了最早的蒸汽机车（火车）之一，它在工厂里拉煤。'

q.en: What was the Sultana disaster described in the book?
q.zh: 书中描述的萨尔特纳号灾难是什么？

options.en: A steamboat whose boiler exploded in 1865, killing over 1,700 people
options.zh: 一艘蒸汽船在1865年锅炉爆炸，造成逾1700人死亡

explain.en: The book says: 'One of the worst disasters in American history took place on April 27, 1865. The boiler on the crowded steamboat Sultana exploded... More than 1,700 people died.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'美国史上最惨重的灾难之一发生于1865年4月27日。拥挤的蒸汽船萨尔特纳号的锅炉爆炸……超过1700人丧生。'

q.en: Until when did steam locomotives run in the United States?
q.zh: 蒸汽机车在美国一直运行到什么时候？

explain.en: The book says: 'Steam locomotives ran in the United States until the 1960s. By then, almost all locomotives had diesel engines.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'蒸汽机车在美国一直运行到20世纪60年代。那时几乎所有机车都已换成柴油机车。'

q.en: How do modern power plants use steam today?
q.zh: 现代发电厂如今如何利用蒸汽？

explain.en: The book says: 'For instance, power plants use it to produce electricity. It powers your lights and your television.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'例如，发电厂利用蒸汽发电。它为你的电灯和电视供电。'

prompt.en: The book says steam power may still have a place in the modern world, including using solar panels to make steam. Do you think steam power has a future? Explain your view.
prompt.zh: 书中说蒸汽动力在现代世界仍有一席之地，包括利用太阳能板产生蒸汽。你认为蒸汽动力有未来吗？解释你的看法。

en: State whether you think steam power has a future
Use a fact from the book, such as power plants or solar steam
Give a reason for your opinion
zh: 说明你是否认为蒸汽动力有未来
运用书中的事实，如发电厂或太阳能蒸汽
给出你持此观点的理由
